steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best explains the concept of torsional heating. A defining explanation must say what torsional heating is, using the notes as evidence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Researchers determined that when both fibers were twisted, the surface temperature of natural rubber increased more than that of dimethicone.: Compares experimental results between two fibers — doesn't define the concept. ✗
  • Choice B — Torsional heating is the process by which an object increases in temperature when it is twisted.: Directly defines the concept by combining notes 1 and 2: twisting (torsion) causes heating. Clear and precise. ✓
  • Choice C — An object's average surface temperature will generally change due to torsion.: Too vague — "change" could mean decrease; the notes specify objects heat up. ✗
  • Choice D — In 2019, researchers studied the effect of torsional heating on various fibers, like dimethicone and natural rubber.: Describes the study, not the concept. ✗
That matches Choice B — Torsional heating is the process by which an object increases in temperature when it is twisted..
